summary: Identifies and characterises five recurring architectural patterns for hybrid classical-quantum systems validated through VQE and QAOA case studies.

subjects: Software Construction; Software Engineering Process; Software Testing
summary: Presents QFlow, a CI/CD framework for quantum software that reduces regression detection time by 63% through noise-aware simulation and statistical flakiness detection.

subjects: Software Quality; Software Engineering Models and Methods
summary: Proposes QualQ, an extension of ISO/IEC 25010 with four quantum-specific quality sub-characteristics and eleven QASM-based metrics validated on 95 GitHub programs.
Catalogues quantum-specific requirement smells and proposes an IEEE 830 extension template with qubit budget, gate depth, noise tolerance, and classical fallback attributes.
Proposes a mutation-testing framework for quantum circuits that adapts classical test adequacy criteria to the quantum domain, achieving 91% defect detection.
